transliteration:
dravya-yajñās tapo-yajñā yoga-yajñās tathāpare |
svādhyāya-jñāna-yajñāś ca yatayaḥ saṃśita-vratāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Some offer wealth, others austerity, some the eight-limbed yoga, others Vedic study and knowledge—all steadfast in vows.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists four ego-syntonic coping styles people use to regulate existential anxiety: material giving, asceticism, embodied mastery, and intellectual pursuit. Each is framed as a ‘sacrifice’ that promises symbolic immortality, yet all remain within the ego’s project of self-enhancement rather than self-transcendence.
psychological_state: Performative virtue—using spiritual practices to bolster a compensatory identity and keep dread at bay.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling Instagram and feeling both admiration and subtle superiority seeing others post their donation receipts, yoga poses, or library stacks.
anchor_text: Every path you cling to still keeps 'you' intact.
